message:
fix(server): batch drain read_buf instead of per-frame, enforce query_buffer_limit on replica stream reads, and use mem::take for pubsub drain

process_buffered_frames: Previously called conn.read_buf.drain(..consumed)
after every frame, which is O(n) memmove per frame in a pipelined burst of
k frames → O(k*n) total. Now accumulates consumed_total and drains once at
the end → O(n) total. This fixes a quadratic performance cliff when clients
pipeline hundreds of commands in a single read.

Replica stream reads: read_frame_from_stream and read_available_stream_bytes
now accept query_buffer_limit and call validate_read_path before extending
the buffer, preventing a misbehaving primary from filling unbounded memory.
All 7 call sites updated (PING/REPLCONF/PSYNC handshake, FULLRESYNC payload,
incremental stream drain). New test
replica_stream_read_path_enforces_query_buffer_limit verifies rejection.

drain_pending_pubsub: Replaced .drain(..).collect() with std::mem::take()
which avoids the intermediate iterator allocation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
